div.div: Effect: A whole apple — skin perfectly intact — falls apart into neat segments at a gentle pull. Method: the preparation happens well before the performance. Thread a needle with strong thread and insert it into the apple through the stem hole. Loop the thread inside the apple through several insertion points, creating internal divisions, then withdraw the needle. The apple looks completely normal. To perform: hold the apple out, grip the stem, and give a firm but smooth pull — the thread segments the flesh cleanly inside while the skin falls open in sections.

The Miraculous Apple is a perfect example of the 'advance preparation' principle in conjuring — the hardest work is done long before the performance, so that in the moment, the performer needs no skill at all. This principle underlies many of magic's most baffling effects.

An apparently whole apple is divided into segments without breaking its skin. The method: a needle and thread are threaded through the apple under the skin in multiple directions before the performance. When the audience asks for the apple to be 'divided,' simply pulling the thread segments the flesh inside while the skin remains intact.

svg.text: Source: The Magician's Own Book (1862) · Heritage Parlor
